source_code:
# source: https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/162636
# repo: home-assistant/core
# title: Ubuntu 24.04 Kernel 6.8.0-100 breaks UDP multiple integrations with PermissionError
# 
# ### The problem
# I encountered issues with multiple integrations (GoodWe, Tuya, Ariston, HACS). Any integration attempting to use UDP or network discovery fails with PermissionError or Network Unreachable. This is a system-wide networking issue affecting the Docker host mode over Wi-Fi.
# ### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?
# core-2026.2.1
# ### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?
# core-2026.2.1
# ### What type of installation are you running?
# Home Assistant Container
# ### Integration causing the issue
# GoodWe, Ariston, HACS, Tuya 
# ### Link to integration documentation on our website
# _No response_
# ### Diagnostics information
# _No response_
# ### Example YAML snippet
# ### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?
# ### Additional informatio

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?
expected_fix_hint: Fix file mode/ownership; run with proper perms or use tempfile.
